title: Innovative Nutty Malaysian Peas Pie
description: A comforting Malaysian dish featuring peas in a nutty, aerated pastry crust, FODMAP-friendly and perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the almond flour, salt, and palm sugar.
Add the coconut oil, eggs, and water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon the peas onto one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the chopped macadamia nuts over the peas.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Brush the top of the pie with a little bit of coconut oil and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pie for 35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
